failure_story: The committee treated management's quarterly liquidation-basis net asset estimate ($11.79 per share) as an auditable, dated floor converging by December 2028, when it was a contingent management mark already embedding an optimistic B2K high-end price ($28.74 million), unresolved Article 78 litigation, and rising entitlement costs. An adverse Article 78 ruling forced a Flowerfield subdivision redesign and delayed any Flowerfield sale; the B2K contract terminated or closed near the $24.0 million low end after years of delay; remaining liquidation and operating costs exceeded the $16.7 million estimate; management raised dilutive capital to fund operations through an extended timeline; and disclosed net assets in liquidation were marked down repeatedly. Thin daily volume (roughly 2,000 to 6,000 shares) prevented exit near quoted prices. Realized distributions fell well below the ~$5.80 entry, turning a modeled 33% annual return into a severe capital loss after years of carry.
earliest_warning_signals: Article 78 appeal on the Flowerfield subdivision remained unresolved as of the 10-Q filed 2026-05-13, and management stated Flowerfield would not be sold until it was decided.

Disclosed net assets in liquidation per share had already fallen about 15% in one year ($13.91 at 12/31/2024 to $11.76 at 12/31/2025) while the target liquidation date had been extended twice from end-2026 to end-2028.
The base case assumed the B2K deal at the $28.74 million high end of a $24.0 million to $28.74 million contingent range, not a contracted closing price.
Management disclosed in the 10-K FY2025 MD&A that additional capital may be needed to fund operations through the 2028 target date.
The 2021 mortgage loan matured October 2026 with extension uncertain, and the 2023 LLYR facility had been refinanced at 15% effective January 2026.
Daily trading volume was often only a few thousand shares, so building or exiting a position near the quoted price was structurally difficult.
The bear scenario still showed a positive annualized return, which understated the open-ended delay and dilution path if litigation and entitlements failed together.
forensic_checks: Track Article 78 docket outcomes and Town of Smithtown B2K site-plan decisions against the disclosed catalyst timeline and company statements that Flowerfield sales wait on the appeal.

Reconcile the three named debt facilities (original bank line, 2021 Rialto mortgage, 2023 LLYR mortgage) to the $10,790,194 balance-sheet total and stress refi, extension, and default scenarios around the October 2026 maturity.
Audit B2K agreement contingencies, amendment history, termination rights, and whether realized proceeds match the high-end NRV already embedded in real estate held for sale.
Roll forward estimated remaining liquidation and operating costs ($16,709,887 as of 3/31/2026) quarter by quarter against actual cash burn and entitlement spending.
Compare each quarterly net-assets-in-liquidation revision to independent Long Island and Westchester industrial/medical-office comparable sales and appraisals.
Monitor dilution events (rights offerings, shelf registrations, new borrowings) against the 2,199,308 share count used in per-share math.
Verify the B2K entitlement option is not counted twice: once inside real estate NRV and again as additive option value.
Re-run short-source and activist scans after Star Equity standstill expiry (December 31, 2026 or 2027) for renewed governance or bear-thesis pressure.
unresolved_items: Article 78 appeal outcome was not confirmed after the 10-Q filed 2026-05-13; the packet cannot prove the subdivision survives.
B2K site-plan approval status with the Town of Smithtown (company expected Q3 2026) was not verified at packet freeze.
Three named debt facilities identified in filings do not fully reconcile to the $10,790,194 loans-payable total on the balance sheet.
No approved third-party or independent appraisal cross-check validates management's liquidation-basis NAV against market transactions.
No public short-seller report was found; a small, illiquid name can still carry undisclosed bear positioning without a published thesis.
Human sign-off on stance was pending given thin volume and unresolved litigation; sizing up before those items cleared was not supported by the packet.
